LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF AMY ESKIN'S MOTION TO SEAL DOCUMENT PREVIOUSLY FILED WITH COURT IS DENIED. INSUFFICIENT CAUSE HAS BEEN SHOWN TO SEAL DOCUMENTS THAT HAVE BEEN PUBLICLY AVAILABLE AS AN EXHIBIT TO THE COMPLAINT IN THIS CASE SINCE FEBRUARY 7, 2013, AND THEN REPEATEDLY RE-FILED IN PROPOSED AND AMENDED PLEADINGS ON MAY 6, 23, AND 31, 2013. FURTHER, THE MOVING PARTY HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE DOCUMENT AT ISSUE, WHICH IS AN EXHIBIT PREPARED FOR THIS LITIGATION, ACTUALLY CONTAINS CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION THAT IS PROTECTED BY UNSPECIFIED CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENTS IN MULTIPLE OTHER CASES. MOVING PARTY CAN ONLY STATE THAT THAT AN ORDER IS NECESSARY TO PREVENT "POSSIBLE DISCLOSURE" "IN THAT THE TOTAL AMOUNT OF THE SETTLEMENT IN EACH CASE COULD BE ESTIMATED BY MAKING AN ASSUMPTION REGARDING THE CONTINGENT FEE PERCENTAGES." (MARSH DECL. IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO FILE DOCUMENT UNDER SEAL, AT 4)(EMPHASIS ADDED). MOVING PARTY HAS FAILED TO SHOW THAT THERE IS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE RECORD, THAT THE OVERRIDING INTEREST SUPPORTS SEALING THE RECORD, AND THAT THERE IS A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY THAT THE OVERRIDING INTEREST WILL BE PREJUDICED IF THE RECORD IS NOW SEALED RETROACTIVELY. (CRC 2.550(D).) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO CSR7489.

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-14-2014 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-05-2013 Set For Trial On Apr-07-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Seal Document Previously Filed With Court
Off Calendar Aug-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF AMY ESKIN's motion To Seal Document Previously Filed With Court IS Denied. Insufficient cause has been shown to seal documents that have been publicly available as an exhibit to the Complaint in this case since February 7, 2013, and then repeatedly re-filed in proposed and amended pleadings on May 6, 23, and 31, 2013. Further, the moving party has not shown that the document at issue, which is an exhibit prepared for this litigation, actually contains confidential information that is protected by unspecified confidentiality agreements in multiple other cases. Moving party can only state that that an order is necessary to prevent "possible disclosure" "in that the total amount of the settlement in each case could be estimated by making an assumption regarding the contingent fee percentages." (Marsh Decl. in support of Motion To File Document Under Seal, at 4)(emphasis added). Moving party has failed to show that there is an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record, that the overriding interest supports sealing the record, and that there is a substantial probability that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is now sealed retroactively. (CRC 2.550(d).) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR7489.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Pltf Amy Eskin
Off Calendar Jul-16-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances on this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant HERSH & HERSH PC'S Motion to Compel the Deposition of Plaintiff Amy Eskin is Off Calendar at the request of the moving party. Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)
